notes.education: Sponsored SB100 creating correspondence school allotments for homeschool families (2013-2014), allowing state funds for educational materials from public/private/religious organizations
notes.fiscal: Cut state budget significantly, defended Permanent Fund Dividend
notes.energy: Strong supporter of oil and gas development in Alaska

profile.education: Misericordia University (BA History 1983), University of Alaska Fairbanks (MEd Education)
profile.background: Spent nearly two decades as teacher, principal, and superintendent in northwest Arctic communities; served in Alaska Senate before election as 12th governor in 2018.

notes.trump_impeachment: One of 7 GOP senators who voted to convict Trump in second impeachment trial (2021). Alaska GOP censured her for this vote
notes.abortion: One of few Republican senators who supports abortion rights
notes.marriage: Voted for Respect for Marriage Act (supporting same-sex marriage codification)
notes.moderate: Described as one of Senate's most moderate Republicans
notes.education: Staunch defender of public education, former PTA president

profile.education: Georgetown University (BA Economics 1980), Willamette College of Law (JD 1985)
profile.background: Longest-serving member of Alaska's congressional delegation. Former Alaska state representative. Appointed to Senate in 2002 by her father, Governor Frank Murkowski. Won 2010 write-in campaign.

notes.defense: Marine Corps veteran, strong national defense advocate
notes.international: Focus on international affairs and armed forces/national security
notes.environment: Mixed record - 15% of bills on environmental protection
notes.native_affairs: 10% of bills relate to Native American issues

profile.education: Harvard University (BA Economics 1987), Georgetown University (JD & MS Foreign Service 1993)
profile.background: Former Alaska Attorney General and Commissioner of Natural Resources. Marine Corps colonel with combat service in Afghanistan. Former White House aide and State Department official.

notes.replaced_peltola: Defeated Democrat Mary Peltola in 2024 under ranked-choice voting
notes.energy: Achieved mandated oil/gas lease sales in ANWR, NPR-A, and Cook Inlet under One Big Beautiful Bill Act
notes.bitcoin: Co-introduced BITCOIN Act with Sen. Lummis to establish Strategic Bitcoin Reserve
notes.second_amendment: Strong 2A supporter, A rating from GOA and NRA

profile.nra_rating: A (also A from Gun Owners of America)
profile.heritage_action: {'note': 'First term, 119th Congress - score still being compiled'}
